ksformat 0.4.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • ksformat Usage Examples

ksformat R Package Development Guide

CRAN=“https://mirror.truenetwork.ru/CRAN/”

IMPORTANT: This project uses Serena MCP for comprehensive knowledge management. Before asking questions or making assumptions:

  1. Check Serena memory first using mcp_oraios_serena_list_memories to see available documentation
  2. Read relevant memory files using mcp_oraios_serena_read_memory before answering questions

Package Purpose

ksformat provides SAS PROC FORMAT-like functionality for R: - Create value-to-label mappings (formats) and label-to-value mappings (invalues) - Proper handling of NA, NULL, NaN, and other missing values - Range-based formatting for numeric data - Global format library for reusable format definitions - Support for both discrete values and ranges

Package Structure

This is a standard R package following the structure defined in R Packages book:

  • R/ - All R function definitions:
    • format_create.R - Create format definitions (value → label)
    • format_apply.R - Apply formats to vectors/data frames
    • format_invalue.R - Reverse formatting (label → value)
    • utilities.R - Helper functions and format library management
  • man/ - Documentation files (.Rd format) for each exported function (auto-generated by roxygen2)
  • DESCRIPTION - Package metadata (name, version, authors, dependencies)
  • NAMESPACE - Controls which functions are exported/imported (auto-generated by roxygen2)

Development Workflow

Essential Commands (from RStudio)

  • Install Package: Ctrl + Shift + B or R CMD INSTALL .
  • Check Package: Ctrl + Shift + E or R CMD check .
  • Test Package: Ctrl + Shift + T (requires testthat)
  • Document: devtools::document() (if using roxygen2)

From Command Line

# Install the package
R CMD INSTALL .

# Check package for CRAN compliance
R CMD check .

# Build source tarball
R CMD build .

Adding New Functions

  1. Create new .R file in R/ directory with function definition

  2. Add roxygen2 comments above function (recommended):

    #' Brief title
#'
#' @param x Description of parameter
#' @return Description of return value
#' @export
#' @examples
#' my_function(x = 5)
my_function <- function(x) {
  # implementation
}

  3. Run devtools::document() to generate .Rd files in man/ and update NAMESPACE

  4. Or manually create .Rd file in man/ if not using roxygen2

Core Functions

Format Creation and Application

  • format_create(..., name, type, default) - Create format (SAS PROC FORMAT equivalent)
    • Discrete: "M" = "Male", "F" = "Female"
    • Ranges: c(0, 18) = "Child", c(18, 65) = "Adult"
    • Special: .missing = "Unknown", .other = "Other"
  • format_apply(x, format, keep_na) - Apply format to vector
    • Handles NA, NULL, NaN automatically
    • Returns character vector with labels
  • format_apply_df(data, ..., suffix, replace) - Apply formats to data frame columns
    • Named arguments: sex = sex_fmt, age = age_fmt

Reverse Formatting (Invalue)

  • format_invalue(..., name, target_type, missing_value) - Create reverse format (SAS INVALUE)

    • Converts labels back to values: "Male" = "M", "Female" = "F"

  • invalue_apply(x, invalue, na_if) - Apply invalue to convert labels to values

  • format_bidirectional(..., name, type) - Create both format and invalue simultaneously

Format Library Management

  • format_register(format, name, overwrite) - Store format in global library
  • format_get(name) - Retrieve format from library
  • format_list() - List all registered formats
  • format_remove(name) - Remove format from library
  • format_clear() - Clear all formats

Utilities

  • is_missing_value(x, include_empty) - Check for NA/NULL/NaN/empty
  • range_spec(low, high, label) - Create range specification
  • format_validate(x, format) - Validate values against format

Usage Examples

# Basic discrete format
sex_fmt <- format_create(
  "M" = "Male",
  "F" = "Female", 
  .missing = "Unknown",
  name = "sex"
)

format_apply(c("M", "F", NA, "X"), sex_fmt)
# [1] "Male"    "Female"  "Unknown" "X"

# Numeric range format
age_fmt <- format_create(
  c(0, 18) = "Child",
  c(18, 65) = "Adult",
  c(65, Inf) = "Senior",
  .missing = "Age Unknown",
  name = "age"
)

format_apply(c(5, 25, 70, NA), age_fmt)
# [1] "Child"       "Adult"       "Senior"      "Age Unknown"

# Reverse formatting
sex_inv <- format_invalue(
  "Male" = "M",
  "Female" = "F",
  "Unknown" = NA
)

invalue_apply(c("Male", "Female", "Unknown"), sex_inv)
# [1] "M" "F" NA

# Format library usage
format_register(sex_fmt)
format_register(age_fmt)
format_list()  # [1] "sex" "age"

# Apply to data frame
df <- data.frame(sex = c("M", "F", NA), age = c(15, 25, 70))
format_apply_df(df, sex = sex_fmt, age = age_fmt)

Missing Value Handling

The package handles missing values in priority order: 1. NA, NULL, NaN → Uses .missing label if defined, otherwise NA 2. Exact matches → Uses defined value-label mapping 3. Range matches → Uses range label (for numeric formats) 4. No match → Uses .other label or returns original value

Special treatment: - keep_na = TRUE in format_apply() preserves NA instead of labeling - na_if parameter in invalue_apply() treats additional values as missing - include_empty = TRUE in is_missing_value() treats empty strings as missing

NAMESPACE Management

Package uses roxygen2 for NAMESPACE generation: - Add #' @export to make function available to users - Add #' @keywords internal to keep helper functions private - Run devtools::document() to regenerate NAMESPACE - Previously used exportPattern("^[[:alpha:]]+") - now switch to explicit exports

Dependencies

When adding package dependencies: 1. Add to DESCRIPTION under Imports: (runtime) or Suggests: (optional/tests) 2. Import specific functions in NAMESPACE or use package::function() notation 3. With roxygen2: use #' @importFrom package function in any R file

Local package installation

R CMD INSTALL --library=~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.5 .

Contents

Developed by Vladimir Larchenko, Igor Aleschenkov.
